// note: Copy this to config.json and edit. host/port is where THIS relay listens (WebSocket + NIP-11 HTTP share the port).
// name/description: Human-readable identity shown to clients via NIP-11.
name: xannyblastr
description: A private gift-wrapped DM relay for me and my web of trust.
// adminNpub: Your identity. npub... or 64-char hex. This pubkey + its web of trust may write.
// relaySecretKey: Identity THIS relay uses to AUTH to downstream relays that require it. nsec... or hex. Run `npm run genkey` to make one. Optional but recommended.
// relaysFile: Path to the human-editable YAML list of manual blast relays. Copy relays.example.yml to this path.
// discoveryRelays: Relays queried to build your web of trust (where kind 3 follow lists are read from).
// outbound rate limiting: Global limiter for outbound WebSocket connections to other relays. Applies to blasting, WoT discovery, and liveness probes.
// 10050 harvesting: 'all' = learn relays from any authorized writer's 10050. 'admin' = only from the admin's own 10050.
// dmRelaySweepDepth: On each WoT refresh, proactively pull kind-10050 DM relay lists and harvest their relays. 1 = admin + direct follows, 2 = + follows-of-follows. Ignored (admin-only) when harvest10050From is 'admin'.
// allowOnionRelays: Harvest and probe .onion relays. Default false (they're unreachable without Tor). Set true only if this process routes traffic through Tor. .local/localhost/loopback are always excluded.
// logRetention: Health-log lifetime before the optional purge+wipe. Default 'off' = logs (and relay success-rate history) are kept indefinitely. Set '7d', '12h', '1w', '30m', or a bare number (= days) to enable periodic wiping. Unhealthy-relay purging runs regardless of this setting.
// read privacy: When true, a client must AUTH and can only read kind-1059 events addressed to its own pubkey.
